title: Apple nachos with peanut butter caramel sauce - Canada.ca
content:
## Apple nachos with peanut butter caramel sauce
- 10 ingredients or less
These delicious apple nachos make a great dessert or afternoon snack. They are ready in minutes and a family favourite.
- 125 mL (½ cup) creamy peanut butter or non-nut alternative
- 60 mL (¼ cup) maple syrup or birch syrup
- 2 mL (½ tsp) vanilla
- 1 mL (¼ tsp) salt (optional)
- 2 medium apples, cored and thinly sliced
- 125 mL (½ cup) lower fat plain Greek yogurt
- 60 mL (¼ cup) peanut butter caramel sauce
- 30 mL (2 tbsp) sunflower seeds (optional)
- 1 mL (¼ tsp) cinnamon (optional)
- Prepare the peanut butter caramel sauce: In a small saucepan, combine all of the ingredients and turn
                the heat to medium. As the ingredients warm, whisk well until the mixture becomes smooth.
- Bring the sauce to a boil, reduce heat and whisk continuously for about 1 minute or until it starts to
                thicken.
- Remove sauce from heat. Set aside.
- Prepare the nachos: On a medium-sized plate, lay your apple slices in a single layer around the outside
                edges of the plate. Layer another small layer on top, over the inside edges, continue until all apple
                slices are used.
- Place Greek yogurt in the center of apple slices, for dipping.
- Drizzle 60 mL (¼ cup) of peanut butter caramel sauce over apple slices.
- Optional: Top with sunflower seeds, sprinkle with cinnamon, and enjoy!
- In an airtight container or mason jar, pour remaining peanut butter caramel sauce and store in the
                refrigerator for 3 to 4 days.
Recipe contributed by the Public Health Agency of Canada, Nutrition North Canada – Nutrition Education
            Initiatives
Learn about food safety .
- Use any fruit you have on hand – bananas or pears would be delicious substitutes!
- Get little chefs involved! Encourage them to add other fun and healthy toppings, such as, shredded
            unsweetened coconut or flax seeds.
- Leftover peanut butter caramel will thicken more as it sits and is refrigerated; gently reheat it or add some
            water to thin it out, if desired.
- Experiment by using this sauce to customize breakfast recipes like classic
                overnight oats or apple
                pie oatmeal .
